summary: Completed backend + frontend verification for S2 streaming integration with polling fallback using public URL. All critical requested flows passed, including WhatsApp/Razorpay webhooks, recent transactions API, and dashboard polling/refresh behavior.

critical.issue: Webhook accepts payload without Razorpay signature verification; endpoint can be spoofed by any caller.

action_items: Add Razorpay webhook signature validation before processing/logging transaction events.
After signature validation is added, rerun webhook regression tests for success and invalid-signature rejection paths.
critical_code_review_comments: S2 fallback behavior is correctly resilient: when S2 token is empty, backend silently skips push and frontend correctly shows polling fallback.
Current webhook handlers are operational, but lack request authenticity checks (especially Razorpay) introduces high-risk data injection path.
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_ prefixed status records and test webhook transaction records (Razorpay + WhatsApp) in preview DB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public URL from frontend/.env used. Pytest suite now includes streaming-focused regression tests in test_transactions_streaming.py. UI verified via browser automation with live webhook trigger and observed 5-second polling fallback updates + refresh button update path.
rca of the issue: Razorpay webhook route directly trusts request body and processes transaction immediately; no signature header validation is performed. Repro: POST arbitrary JSON to /api/webhook/razorpay returns 200 and persists transaction. Mitigation: verify X-Razorpay-Signature against request payload using webhook secret and reject invalid signatures with 401/400.
